finding.description: The search method uses raw SQL queries, which can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
finding.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM methods to safely query the database.
tests: Test for SQL injection by attempting to manipulate the search query.

finding.description: The Vendor model uses $fillable instead of $guarded, violating the convention for mass assignment protection.
finding.recommendation: Change the model to use $guarded = ['id']; instead of $fillable.
tests: Test that mass assignment works correctly with the new guarded setting.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-02 for mass assignment conventions.

finding.description: The search view uses {!! !!} for outputting vendor names, which can lead to XSS vulnerabilities if not properly sanitized.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-03] XSS vulnerability in search view
finding.recommendation: Change {!! $vendor->name !!} to {{ $vendor->name }} to ensure proper escaping.
tests: Test that the output is properly escaped and does not allow XSS.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-03 for XSS prevention.

finding.description: The store method does not validate the request data before processing.
finding.recommendation: Add $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity and security.
tests: Test that invalid data returns a validation error.
Verify that valid data creates a purchase order.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the HasCreator trait, which is required for tracking the creator of the model instance.
finding.recommendation: Implement the HasCreator trait in the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the created_by field is populated correctly.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-07 for audit requirements.

finding.description: The Vendor model is missing the explicit $table property with the escm_ prefix, violating the convention for model definitions.
finding.recommendation: Add the property protected $table = 'escm_vendors'; to the Vendor model.
tests: Test that the model correctly references the database table.
finding.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-10 for model conventions.

finding.description: The index method does not call checkAccessPermission() and lacks multi-tenant isolation with ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()).
finding.recommendation: Add checkAccessPermission() at the start of the index method and use ESCM::scopeProject(auth()->user()) for data retrieval.
tests: Test that unauthorized access is blocked.
Verify that only relevant purchase orders are returned based on user permissions.

finding.description: The update_status method combines multiple non-CRUD actions in a single method.
finding.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-12] Non-CRUD actions in generic method
finding.recommendation: Separate non-CRUD actions into distinct methods.
tests: Verify that each action (approve, release, propose) has its own method.

finding.description: The syncFromSap method does not use a dedicated sync service and does not extend Base.
finding.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q05] External data without sync service
finding.recommendation: Implement a dedicated sync service that extends App\Sync\Service\Base for syncing data from SAP.
tests: Verify that the sync functionality works with the new service.

finding.description: The store method logs sensitive data (password, pin) without masking.
finding.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q08] Logging sensitive data without masking
finding.recommendation: Remove sensitive data from logs or mask it before logging.
tests: Check logs to ensure sensitive data is not recorded.

fp_findings.description: The store method does not validate the incoming request data, which can lead to invalid data being saved to the database.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [WIKA-Q02] Missing validation in store method
fp_findings.recommendation: Add validation to the store method using $request->validate([...]) to ensure data integrity.
tests: Test that invalid data returns a validation error.
Test that valid data is saved correctly.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule WIKA-Q02 for validation requirements.

fp_findings.description: The search method uses a raw SQL query, which violates the convention of using Eloquent ORM for database access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-01] Raw SQL query in search method
fp_findings.recommendation: Use Eloquent ORM to perform the search instead of a raw SQL query.
tests: Test that the search returns correct results using Eloquent.
Test that the search handles special characters safely.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-01 for database access conventions.

fp_findings.description: The export method does not check for user permissions before exporting vendor data, which can lead to unauthorized access.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-11] Missing permission check in export method
fp_findings.recommendation: Implement a permission check using checkAccessPermission() at the start of the export method.
tests: Test that unauthorized users cannot access the export functionality.
Test that authorized users can successfully export data.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-11 for authorization requirements.

fp_findings.description: The Vendor model is missing the ActivityLogged trait, which is required for tracking changes to the model instance.
fp_findings.recommendation: Implement the ActivityLogged trait in the Vendor model.
tests: Test that changes to the model are logged correctly.
fp_findings.notes: Refer to rule CTDL-08 for audit requirements.

fp_findings.description: The detail method does not follow the Citadel page lifecycle.
fp_findings.check_name: SAST | [CTDL-06] Does not follow Citadel Page lifecycle
fp_findings.recommendation: Follow the Citadel page lifecycle: ComponentsPage::make()->view()->business()->schema()->render() in the detail method.
tests: Verify that the page lifecycle is correctly implemented.

fp_findings.description: The callback method is vulnerable to open redirect attacks.
fp_findings.recommendation: Validate the redirect URL to ensure it is safe before redirecting.
tests: Test for open redirect by providing a malicious URL.
